title: thymus serpyllum var. subcitratus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. subcitratus, commonly known as wild thyme, is a type of flowering plant in the mint family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which have been found to have a range of medicinal applications, including the treatment of respiratory issues and digestive problems. The health benefits of wild thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Wild thyme can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
